---
title: "Phase Loop Contracts"
---

<div class="claim-boundary-strip">
<span class="claim-boundary-label">Claim boundary</span>
<span class="claim-boundary-badge claim-boundary-proved">proved finite loop sums</span>
<span class="claim-boundary-badge claim-boundary-executable">phase diagnostics</span>
<span class="claim-boundary-badge claim-boundary-experimental">dynamics separate</span>
<span class="claim-boundary-badge claim-boundary-speculative">no physics claim</span>
</div>

Loop phases connect winding, holonomy, vortex-charge vocabulary, and
Kuramoto-style phase locking. The literature relates phase-locked solutions to
topological winding numbers and loop currents, including
[Delabays, Coletta, and Jacquod](https://arxiv.org/abs/1512.04266). Circle
Calculus only formalizes the finite bookkeeping:

```text
loop_charge = sum(increments) mod period
reverse_loop_charge = -loop_charge
phase_locked(left, right) = left mod period = right mod period
```

## Lean Surface

```lean
import Circle.Applications.PhaseLoop
import Circle.Applications.Public
```

The theorem ids are `CC-T0161` through `CC-T0172`, covering loop-charge empty,
singleton, append, reverse, vortex-charge alias and reversal, closed-loop
endpoint-gauge cancellation, finite phase locking, and winding/residue
reconstruction.

## Python Example

```python
from circle_math.core import phase_lock_report, phase_loop_report

loop = phase_loop_report(12, [3, 4, 7], base_gauge=5)
print(loop.charge)
print(loop.reverse_charge)
print(loop.charge_plus_reverse)
print(loop.closed_loop_gauge_invariant)

locked = phase_lock_report(12, [1, 13, 25])
print(locked.all_locked)
print(locked.order_parameter)
```

The Lean-backed fields are finite modular identities. The order parameter is an
executable circular-statistics diagnostic, not a proof of oscillator dynamics,
stability, or synchronization threshold.

These diagnostics are not proof of a physical model. The theorem ids are the
status source for the finite modular statements, and dynamics remain separate
from Lean proof status.
